authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife
U.S. Geological Survey (Geographic Names Information System)

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.40(b)(71) — Pajaro River (Monterey, Santa Clara, Santa Cruz and San Benito Cos.)
sources.quotedText: (71) Pajaro River (Monterey, Santa Clara, Santa Cruz and San Benito Cos.) from mouth to Uvas Creek. Also see Section 8.00(c) Low-Flow Restrictions. Dec. 1 through Mar. 7, but only on Sat., Sun., Wed., legal holidays and opening and closing days. Only barbless hooks may be used. 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.40 — Hatchery Trout/Steelhead Footnote (**) Definition
sources.quotedText: Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released.

sources.title: CDFW — Low Flow Fishing Regulations
sources.quotedText: Shall be closed to all fishing when the department determines that flows are impeding fish passage.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 230242, "Pajaro River", Stream, Monterey/Santa Clara/Santa Cruz/San Benito Cos., CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":230242,"gaz_name":"Pajaro River","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","state_alpha":"CA"} — geometry multipoint; first point (Web Mercator -13559792.3772, 4418213.6616) converts to lon -121.809687, lat 36.849960, at the river's mouth into Monterey Bay near Moss Landing/Pajaro Dunes, correcting the file's prior coordinate (-121.8110, 36.8680) which sat ~2.0km northeast and did not match the GNIS-derived point.

parameters.note: Open only on Saturdays, Sundays, Wednesdays, legal holidays, and the opening and closing days of the season; closed all other days within the Dec. 1 - Mar. 7 window. Applies from the mouth to Uvas Creek. Also subject to Low-Flow Restrictions, Section 8.00(c).

regulations.humanSummary: Open December 1 through March 7, but only on Saturdays, Sundays, Wednesdays, legal holidays, and opening/closing days, from the mouth to Uvas Creek
regulations.verbatimText: Dec. 1 through Mar. 7, but only on Sat., Sun., Wed., legal holidays and opening and closing days.

parameters.note: Only barbless hooks may be used.

regulations.humanSummary: Only barbless hooks may be used
regulations.verbatimText: Only barbless hooks may be used.

parameters.counting_rule: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead per day, combined; 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead in possession, combined.

regulations.humanSummary: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead daily bag limit; 4 in possession
regulations.verbatimText: 2 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead **. 4 hatchery trout or hatchery steelhead ** in possession.

parameters.condition: Trout or steelhead lacking a healed adipose fin clip (i.e. wild/unmarked fish)
parameters.note: Only hatchery-marked fish (healed adipose fin clip) may be retained; all other trout and steelhead must be released immediately unharmed.

regulations.humanSummary: Only adipose-fin-clipped hatchery trout/steelhead may be kept; unmarked wild fish must be released immediately.
regulations.verbatimText: Hatchery trout or steelhead in anadromous waters are those showing a healed adipose fin clip (adipose fin is absent). Unless otherwise provided, all other trout and steelhead must be immediately released.

parameters.note: Also see Low-Flow Restrictions, Section 8.00(c): closed to all fishing when the department determines that flows are impeding fish passage.

regulations.humanSummary: Also subject to low-flow closure: closed to all fishing when flows are determined to be impeding fish passage

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
